name: Chechen Republic of Ichkeria (1991–2000)

colors.symbolism: the lower stripe and the blood of those who died

description: Three horizontal stripes — green (top, broader), white (narrow), red (bottom) — with a yellow wolf lying beneath a yellow crescent moon and stars on the central white stripe.
history: Flag of the Chechen Republic of Ichkeria, the de-facto independent state declared in 1991 in the wake of the Soviet collapse. The state was suppressed during the First and Second Chechen Wars (ending in 2000). The wolf is a Chechen national symbol and the green-white-red colours signal both Islamic identity (green) and revolutionary tradition.
